Free Text: This area is prone to contamination by items thrown onto the track and can include used syringes/needles. Areas under overbridges are at particular risk, although it could occur anywhere throughout this mileage range. If undertaking any activity outwith the ballasted area extreme care should be taken to avoid injury from these hazards. Control measures as a minimum should include adequate site lighting if in the hours of darkness, use of mechanical aids to lift any material or fly tipping, and adequate PPE with special consideration to lower limbs and hand/arms. If undertaking any activity within the ballasted area ensure that a detailed visual check is made of the area before picking up anything from the track. If any needles are found to be present, avoid and report immediately through Control for proper disposal.

ELR Name: MUIRHOUSE SOUTH JN - KILMARNOCK GB&K JN (GB&K LINE)
Free Text:
From the B7064 Western Road northbound, turn right into Munro Avenue <Grid 419.387>. After 200 yards, turn left into the industrial estate, right into the Regional Plant Workshops, then left along the road to the vehicle park. The signalbox is located in the ~V~ formed by the Kilmarnock/Glasgow and Kilmarnock/Barassie lines. Entry to the signalbox is via the main door located on the side away from the vehicle park, on the up side of the Kilmarnock to Barassie line.
 NB:There is no direct walking route to or from Kilmarnock signalbox via the lineside cess. Access is via Western Road only.

ELR Name: W.C.M.L.(CARLISLE - ELR CHANGE LAW JN)
Free Text:
Pantograph Monitoring System cameras have been installed at Braidwood on the footbridge and will be working from 18th January 2020.  The camera is located on the footbridge and contains lasers to be able to scan the pantograph.  The cameras contains 2 types of lasers: ~
LDS30 Laser Ranges~
- The laser ranges are used for distance measurements and for identifying when a train/pantograph is under the scanner. ~
- The laser ranges are Laser Class 1 and therefore considered eye safe under all conditions. ~
ZQ1 Line Laser ~
- The line laser emits a laser line with an angel of 60 degrees.~
- The line laser is not to be considered eye safe at the emitting point of the laser.  However since the laser spreads its energy in an angle, the intensity per area decreases with the distance. ~
~
It has been agreed that when work is taking place using machines underneath the cameras the cameras should be turned off.  This can be done using the isolation switch on structure number G6 10 15.

Free Text: SCOTLAND ROUTE HAS INTRODUCED A PROHIBITION ON THE USE OF WARNING SSOW WORKING UNLESS PROTECTION IS PROVIDED BY LOWS OR TOWS. THIS INCLUDES WORKING AS AN INDIVIDUAL WORKING ALONE OR GOING DOWN THE HIERARCHY WHEN PROTECTION SSOW IS OR BECOMES UNAVAILABLE. YOU WILL STILL BE ALLOWED TO "CROSS THE LINE" OR GO ROUND AN OBSTACLE AS LONG AS YOUR PRIMARY SSOW IS A PROTECTION SSOW AND YOU HAVE SIGHTING DISTANCE AND HAVE A WAY OF CALCULATING THIS DISTANCE. DO NOT ACCEPT ANY PLAN THAT HAS WARNING SSOW AS THE PRIMARY SSOW UNLESS LOWS OR TOWS ARE UTILISED.
